op

Phase 1 & 2: Plan Before You Build

Phase 1: Study the Target

Use the Sound-Alike Method to study professional songs in your style. Analyze structure, section length, instrument choices, energy changes, transitions, hooks, and mix balance.

Ask: What makes the chorus bigger than the verse? How does the song build and release tension?

study more references first.”

Student Rule: “Tf you do not know what your song should do,

Phase 2: Create the Song Blueprint ———

Place markers in your DAW for every section: intro, verse, pre-chorus, chorus, bridge, drop, outro, or any sections your song needs.

For each section, decide the instruments, their roles, the energy level, and the emotional purpose.

This map becomes the production plan for the whole song.

Student Rule: “Do not build the fuil song until the blueprint is finished.”

—!—— Why This Matters ——

Planning first reduces confusion, prevents random decisions, and gives the entire session a clear direction.
